classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After these things Jesus walked in Galilee; for He would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now the Jews’ feast of tabernacles was at hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing is worse than envy and malice; through these death entered into the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when the devil saw man honored, he endured not his prosperity, but used every means to destroy him. ( Wisd. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And from the same root one may everywhere see this same fruit produced.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus Abel was slain; thus David, with many other just men, was like to have been so; from this also the Jews became Christ-slayers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And declaring this the Evangelist said, “After these things Jesus walked in Galilee; for He had not power to walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What sayest thou, O blessed John?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had not He “power,” who was able to do all that He would?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that said, “Whom seek ye?” ( c. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ) and cast them backward?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who was present, yet not seen ( c. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4 ), had not He “power”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then afterwards did He come among them in the midst of the temple, in the midst of the feast, when there was an assembly, when they that longed for murder were present, and utter those sayings which enraged them yet the more?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yea, this at least men marveled at, saying, “Is not this He, whom they seek to kill?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, lo, He speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto Him.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What mean these riddles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Away with the word!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist spake not so that he might be supposed to utter riddles, but to make it plain that He showeth proofs both of His Godhead and His Manhood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when he saith, that “He had not power,” he speaketh of Him as a man, doing many things after the manner of men; but when he saith, that He stood in the midst of them, and they seized Him not, he showeth to us the power of the Godhead, (as man He fled, as God He appeared,) and in both cases he speaks truly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To be in the midst of those who were plotting against Him, and yet not be seized by them, showed His unrivaled and irresistible nature; to yield strengthened and authenticated the Dispensation, that neither Paul of Samosata, nor Marcion, nor those affected with their maladies, might have anything to say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this then he stoppeth all their mouths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After these things was the Jews’ feast of tabernacles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The words, “after these things,” mean only, that the writer has here been concise, and has passed over a long interval of time, as is clear from this circumstance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When Christ sat on the mountain, he saith, that it was the feast of the Passover; while here the writer mentions the “feast of tabernacles,” and during the five months hath neither related or taught us anything else, except the miracle of the loaves, and the sermon made to those who ate them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He ceased not to work miracles, and to converse, both in the day, and in the evening, and oftentimes at night; at least, it was thus that He presided over His disciples, as all the Evangelists tell us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then have they omitted that interval?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because it was impossible to recount everything fully, and moreover, because they were anxious to mention those points which were followed by any fault-finding or gainsaying of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There were many circumstances like those which here are omitted; for that He raised the dead, healed the sick, and was admired, they have frequently recorded; but when they have anything uncommon to tell, when they have to describe any charge seemingly put forth against Him, these things they set down; such as this now, that “His brethren believed Him not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a circumstance like this brings with it no slight suspicion, and it is worth our while to admire their truth-loving disposition, how they are not ashamed to relate things which seem to bring disgrace upon their Teacher, but have been even more anxious to report these than other matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For instance, the writer having passed by many signs and wonders and sermons, has sprung at once to this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, saith he, “His brethren said unto Him, Depart hence, and go into Judæa, that Thy disciples also may see the works that Thou doest; for there is no man that doeth anything in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Show thyself to the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither did His brethren believe in Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What unbelief, saith some one, is here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They exhort Him to work miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is great deed; for of unbelief come their words, and their insolence, and their unseasonable freedom of speech.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they thought, that owing to their relationship, it was lawful for them to address Him boldly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And their request seems forsooth to be that of friends, but the words were those of great maliciousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in this place they reproach Him with cowardice and vainglory: since to say, “no man doeth anything in secret,” is the expression of persons charging Him with cowardice, and suspecting the things done by Him as being not really done; and to add, that “he seeketh to be known,” was to accuse Him of vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But observe, I pray you, the power of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of those who said these things, one became first Bishop of Jerusalem, the blessed James, of whom Paul saith, “Other of the Apostles saw I none, save James, the Lord’s brother” ( Gal. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ); and Judas also is said to have been a marvelous man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet these persons had been present also at Cana, when the wine was made, but as yet they profited nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence then had they so great unbelief?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From their evil mind, and from envy; for superiority among kindred is wont somehow to be envied by such as are not alike exalted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But who are those that they call disciples here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The crowd that followed Him, not the twelve.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how mildly He answered; He said not, “Who are ye that counsel and instruct Me thus?” but,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My time is not yet come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He here seemeth to me to hint at something other than He expresseth; perhaps in their envy they designed to deliver Him up to the Jews; and pointing out this to them, He saith, “My time is not yet come,” that is, “the time of the Cross and the Death, why then hasten ye to slay Me before the time?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But your time is always ready.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “Though ye be ever with the Jews, they will not slay you who desire the same things with them; but Me they will straightway wish to kill.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that it is ever your time to be with them without danger, but My time is when the season of the Cross is at hand, when I must die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that this was His meaning, He showed by what followed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The world cannot hate you;” (how should it hate those who desire, and who run for the same objects as itself?) “but Me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That is, because I upbraid and rebuke it, therefore I am hated.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this let us learn to master our anger, and not to give way to unworthy passion, though they be mean men who give us counsel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if Christ meekly bore with unbelievers counseling Him, when their counsel was improper and not from any good intention, what pardon shall we obtain, who being but dust and ashes, yet are annoyed with those who counsel us, and deem that we are unworthily treated, although the persons who do this may be but a little humbler than ourselves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe in this instance how He repelleth their accusation with all gentleness; for when they say, “Show Thyself to the world,” He replieth, “The world cannot hate you, but Me the world hateth”; thus removing their accusation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So far,” He saith, “am I from seeking honor from men, that I cease not to reprove them, and this when I know that by this course hatred is produced against and death prepared for Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And where,” asketh some one, “did He rebuke men?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When did He ever cease to do so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He not say, “Think not that I will accuse you to the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is one that accuseth you, even Moses.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again; “I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you”: and “How can ye believe, who receive honor from men, and seek not the honor that cometh from God only?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He hath everywhere shown, that it was the open rebuke, not the violation of the Sabbath, which caused the hatred against Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore doth He send them to the feast, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Go ye up to the feast: I go not up yet”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that He said these things not as needing them, or desiring to be flattered by them, but permitting them to do what pertained to Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” saith some one, “went He up after saying, ‘I go not up’?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, once for all, “I go not up,” but, “now,” that is, “not with you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For My time is not yet fulfilled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet He was about to be crucified at the coming Passover.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then went He not up also? for if He went not up because the time was not yet come, He ought not to have gone up at all.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He went not up for this purpose, that He might suffer, but that He might instruct them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But wherefore secretly? since He might by going openly both have been amidst them, and have restrained their unruly impulses as He often did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was because He would not do this continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since had He gone up openly, and again blinded them, He would have made His Godhead to shine through in a greater degree, which at present behooved not, but He rather concealed it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And since they thought that His remaining was from cowardice, He showeth them the contrary, and that it was from confidence, and a dispensation, and that knowing beforehand the time when He should suffer, He would, when it should at length be at hand, be most desirous of going up to Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And methinks by saying, “Go ye up,” He meant, “Think not that I compel you to stay with Me against your will,” and this addition of, “My time is not yet fully come,” is the expression of one declaring that miracles must be wrought and sermons spoken, so that greater multitudes might believe, and the disciples be made more steadfast by seeing the boldness and the sufferings of their Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Learn we then, from what hath been said, His kindness and gentleness; “Learn of Me, for I am meek and lowly of heart” ( Matt. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 29 ); and let us cast away all bitterness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any exalt himself against us, let us be humble; if any be bold, let us wait upon him; if any bite and devour us with mocks and jests, let us not be overcome; lest in defending ourselves we destroy ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For wrath is a wild beast, a wild beast keen and angry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then repeat to ourselves soothing charms drawn from the holy Scripture, and say, “Thou art earth and ashes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why is earth and ashes proud?” ( Ecclus. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ), and, “The sway of his fury shall be his destruction” ( Ecclus. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 22 ): and, “The wrathful man is not comely” ( Prov. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 , LXX.); for there is nothing more shameful, nothing uglier than a visage inflamed with anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As when you stir up mud there is an ill savor, so when a soul is disturbed by passion there is great indecency and unpleasantness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “I endure not insult from mine enemies.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore? tell me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If the charge be true, then thou oughtest, even before the affront, to have been pricked at heart, and thank thine enemy for his rebukes; if it be false, despise it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath called thee poor, laugh at him; he hath called thee base-born and foolish, then mourn for him; for “He that saith to his brother, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whenever therefore one insults thee, consider the punishment that he undergoeth; then shalt thou not only not be angry, but shalt even shed tears for him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For no man is wroth with one in a fever or inflammation, but pities and weeps for all such; and such a thing is a soul that is angry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, if even thou desire to avenge thyself, hold thy peace, and thou hast dealt thine enemy a mortal blow; while if thou addest reviling to reviling, thou hast kindled a fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “the bystanders accuse us of weakness if we hold our peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No, they will not condemn your weakness, but admire you for your wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, if you are stung by insolence, you become insolent; and being stung, compel men to think that what hath been said of you is true.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, tell me, doth a rich man laugh when he is called poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it not because he is conscious that he is not poor? if therefore we will laugh at insults, we shall afford the strongest proof that we are not conscious of the faults alleged.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, how long are we to dread the accounts we render to men? how long are we to despise our common Lord, and be nailed to the flesh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For whereas there is among you strife, and envying, and divisions, are ye not carnal?” ( 1 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then become spiritual, and bridle this dreadful wild beast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Anger differs nothing from madness, it is a temporary devil, or rather it is a thing worse than having a devil; for one that hath a devil may be excused, but the angry man deserves ten thousand punishments, voluntarily casting himself into the pit of destruction, and before the hell which is to come suffering punishment from this already, by bringing a certain restless turmoil and never silent storm of fury, through all the night and through all the day, upon the reasonings of his soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us therefore, that we may deliver ourselves from the punishment here and the vengeance hereafter, cast out this passion, and show forth all meekness and gentleness, that we may find rest for our souls both here and in the Kingdom of Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To which may we all attain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Spirit be glory, now and ever and world without end.
